title: Crepinella spruceana
taxonomy: Crepinella spruceana is classified within the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the filmy fern family. This family includes about 400 species of epiphytic or terrestrial ferns. Crepinella spruceana is a species of fern endemic to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is typically found growing on trees or rocks in shaded and humid environments. The classification of Crepinella spruceana is further refined to the genus Crepinella, which is characterized by its small, delicate fronds.
uses: Crepinella spruceana is reportedly used in traditional medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
